name: Music Video
description: Beat-synced music video / lyric video / performance clip in 9:16 or 16:9. The song is the spine: every visual change is anchored to a kick, snare, hi-hat, vocal line, or harmonic shift. Three strategy modes — Performance (artist front and center), Narrative (story unfolds with the track), Abstract/Visualizer (no performer, pure motion). Genre dictates color, lighting, and cut rate; song structure (verse / build / chorus / drop / bridge / outro) dictates energy arc. Reference is optional — if the user supplies a track or a BPM target, the editor locks cuts to it; if not, the template generates to a synthetic beat grid. ElevenLabs Music can compose an original track when the user has lyrics or a vibe but no song.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one keyframe per scene; reference image (artist photo, location reference, mood board) optional but improves consistency across scenes
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5s clips. Cuts are timed in composition to align with beat grid; generate_audio: false (audio comes from the track, not the clip).
stackSummary.voiceover: None by default — the song is the audio. For lyric-video variants, captions render synced lyrics. Optional spoken intro/outro via ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2.
stackSummary.captions: Lyric mode: KaraokeCaptions (per-word highlight, synced to vocal track). Visualizer mode: no captions or sparse hook-line drops via MinimalCaptions.
stackSummary.music: Two paths: (a) user supplies the track (mp3/wav uploaded as reference) — the editor extracts BPM and beat grid via ffmpeg/aubio and locks cuts to it; (b) user has no track — ElevenLabs Music composes one from a genre + mood + duration brief. BPM target drives cut rate.

constants.structure: intro → verse → build → chorus/drop → bridge → final-chorus → outro
constants.cutRateRule: minimum one visual change per 2 bars on verse, one per bar on chorus, one per beat on drop

variationAxes: genre (hip-hop, pop, rock, EDM, R&B, lo-fi, jazz, classical, country, K-pop)
color palette (genre-dictated; see prompt-cookbook.md genre-mood matrix)
performer count (solo / duo / band / dance crew / no-performer)
aspect ratio (9:16 vertical for short-form / 16:9 horizontal for YouTube)
doNotCopyLiterally: Genre conventions, hook patterns, beat-sync vocabulary. Vibe reference only — do not lift specific scenes, performers, or compositions from any one example.
referenceNotes: Optional but strongly recommended: (1) a reference track (mp3/wav) — unlocks real BPM extraction and locked cut grid; (2) artist photo if the video features a real performer (then reference-required gate applies — no AI-improvised faces of named real people); (3) genre/style reference videos (URLs to similar music videos for vibe match). Without any reference the template generates an abstract visualizer to a synthesized beat at user-specified BPM.
